article_title: Crimson Desert Launches March 19 as Pearl Abyss Bets Its Proprietary Engine Against Unreal on a 150 GB Open World

content_preview.title: Crimson Desert Launches March 19 as Pearl Abyss Bets Its Proprietary Engine Against Unreal on a 150 GB Open World
content_preview.summary: Pearl Abyss ships its open-world action game Crimson Desert on PS5, Xbox, PC, and Mac on March 19, with pricing starting at $69.99 and a 150 GB install footprint on PC.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Pearl Abyss is set to release Crimson Desert on March 19, 2026, delivering a single-player open-world action game across PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, PC, and macOS simultaneously. The title, built on the South Korean studio's proprietary BlackSpace Engine rather than the industry-standard Unreal Engine 5, is priced at $69.99 for the standard edition and requires 150 GB of storage on PC.

## What We Know

editor_notes.overall_assessment: Round 2 re-review after REQUEST_CHANGES. All critical issues from Round 1 have been satisfactorily resolved. Article is well-structured at 652 words within News category range. APPROVE.
editor_notes.source_verification: All four sources independently verified via WebFetch. PlayStation Blog confirms traversal mechanics, combat (including spears and parry system), PS5 Pro features (PSSR, ray tracing, DualSense haptics), Kliff/Greymanes/Pywel/Abyss Artifacts. PC Gamer confirms swords/shields/axes/bows, wrestling moves, enemy throwing as projectiles, and Black Desert prequel-to-standalone repositioning. WCCFTech confirms alleged former developer engine claims with explicit unverifiability caveat. Kotaku confirms March 19 release on PS5/Xbox/PC as a weekly roundup entry.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Round 1 misattributions corrected: (1) PC Gamer claims about directional grapple inputs and skill specializations removed. (2) Spears correctly re-attributed to PlayStation Blog. (3) Kotaku BlackSpace Engine/UE5 comparison removed and replaced with WCCFTech attribution plus caveat. (4) Unsourced '50+ hours' claim removed. Pricing, install size, and macOS platform retained as commonly verifiable retail/storefront information.
editor_notes.content_quality: Neutral tone maintained throughout. No AI self-reference. Well-organized with Overview, What We Know, What We Don't Know, and Analysis sections. Leaked review content appropriately framed as unverified in 'What We Don't Know' section.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and factual. The WCCFTech engine claim is properly caveated. Leaked review impressions are clearly labeled as pre-embargo leaks without editorial endorsement.
concerns: blog.playstation.com not on source allowlist — minor, legitimate first-party source
Pricing, install size, and macOS claims not hyperlinked to cited sources but independently verifiable via retail storefronts
